articles.title: HM Amit Shah reviews preparadness for heatwave, floods - BW Police World
articles.full_text:
New Delhi, May 12
Union Home Minister and Minister of Cooperation, Amit Shah, reviewed country's preparedness to deal with potential floods and heat waves in a comprehensive manner during an important high-level meeting held in New Delhi.
Addressing the meeting, HM Amit Shah said that at least 60 lakes should be included in the plan to develop an early warning system for 30 high-risk lakes in Jammu & Kashmir, Ladakh, Uttarakhand, Himachal Pradesh, Arunachal Pradesh, and Sikkim with the support of the National Disaster Management Authority (NDMA). Shah also said that there should be an integrated system for flood forecasting at both the Centre and State level.
Shah said that Flood Crisis Management Teams (FCMTs) should be constituted and activated in every state of the country. He said that the guidelines issued by the National Disaster Management Authority (NDMA) for disasters have led to better awareness and the development of a “Whole of Government” approach, but reviewing compliance with these guidelines at the state, district, and municipal levels can further strengthen their implementation. He also said that the NDMA should conduct a study to assess how many states are following the Ministry of Home Affairs’ directives and the NDMA guidelines for dealing with forest fires, heat waves, and floods.
Union Home Minister said under the leadership of Prime Minister Narendra Modi, efforts must be made to implement the vision of Zero Casualty Disaster Management. He said that greater possibilities for water conservation and improvement in groundwater levels should be explored through water storage and check dam projects. Our objective should be to conserve water by constructing check dams on rivers while also minimizing the impact of heat waves. He further said that the CAMPA Fund should be utilized to make efforts aimed at maintaining environmental balance more multi-dimensional.
Shah said that a master plan should be prepared to address the changes in weather patterns caused by climate change and the growing disaster-related challenges arising from them by adopting a “Whole of Government” and “Whole of Society” approach. He asked the officials of the ministries and departments present in the meeting to focus on consolidating and improving the existing apps and portals instead of creating new ones. He also said that weather forecasts and warnings should be disseminated widely and effectively.

articles.title: Heavy to very heavy rainfall, hailstorm and heatwave warning issued for several states | To read the entire bulletin issued by IMD | Inshorts - Inshorts
articles.full_text: The IMD has issued heavy to very heavy rainfall, hailstorm and heatwave warning for several states/UT for Wednesday. Heavy to very heavy rainfall is very likely over Assam, Meghalaya, West Bengal, Sikkim, Andaman and Nicobar Islands and Arunachal Pradesh. Hailstorm is very likely over Himachal Pradesh and Uttarakhand. Heatwave is very likely over Gujarat, Maharashtra, Rajasthan and Madhya Pradesh.

articles.title: Weather Warning: IMD Issues Heavy Rain, Thunderstorm and Heatwave Alert Across India; Check State-Wise Forecast - Dailyhunt
articles.full_text:
India is likely to witness a major weather shift over the next few days as a strong Western Disturbance and multiple cyclonic circulations become active across different regions.
The latest Weather Warning issued by the India Meteorological Department (IMD) predicts heavy rain, thunderstorms, lightning, gusty winds, snowfall in hilly areas, and heatwave conditions in several states between May 11 and May 16, 2026.
From North India to the southern coastal states, changing weather conditions are expected to impact daily life. While many regions may receive relief from rising temperatures through rainfall, some states are likely to continue facing intense heatwave conditions.
Western Disturbance To Trigger Rain and Storm Activity in North India
The active Western Disturbance is expected to bring widespread rain and thunderstorm activity across northern states over the coming days.
Jammu & Kashmir, Ladakh to Witness Rain and Snowfall
Light to moderate rain and snowfall are likely in parts of Jammu & Kashmir and Ladakh from May 11 to May 13. Strong winds reaching speeds of 40-60 km/h, along with thunder and lightning, may also affect several areas during this period.
Himachal Pradesh and Uttarakhand Under Heavy Rain Alert
Heavy rainfall accompanied by thunderstorms is expected in Himachal Pradesh between May 11 and May 15. Uttarakhand may also experience intense showers and storm activity from May 11 to May 14. Residents in hilly regions have been advised to stay alert due to possible weather-related disruptions.
Uttar Pradesh Weather Forecast
Weather conditions in Uttar Pradesh are expected to change significantly over the next few days.
- Western Uttar Pradesh may receive light to moderate rainfall between May 12 and 13.
- Eastern Uttar Pradesh is likely to witness rain activity between May 13 and 14.
Thunderstorms, lightning strikes, and strong winds are also expected to accompany the rainfall in many districts.
Delhi Weather Update: Light Rain Likely Till May 13
Delhi is expected to see cloudy skies and intermittent rain over the next three days.
Delhi Forecast Highlights
- May 11: Possibility of thunderstorms with gusty winds of 30-50 km/h
- May 12: Light rainfall with continued strong winds
- May 13: Overcast sky with chances of evening thunderstorms and light showers
The changing weather may provide temporary relief from the prevailing heat in the national capital.
Rajasthan Faces Dual Threat of Heatwave and Dust Storms
Rajasthan is likely to experience contrasting weather conditions this week.
Heatwave Alert Continues
Western Rajasthan and isolated parts of Gujarat may face heatwave conditions from May 11 to May 14.
Eastern Rajasthan is expected to remain under heatwave conditions from May 11 to May 13.
Dust Storm and Rain Expected
Due to the Western Disturbance, dust storms and light rain are likely in the Bikaner Division, Shekhawati region, and Jaipur-Bharatpur belt on May 11 and 12.
Northeast India To Receive Rain and Thunderstorms
Several northeastern states are also likely to witness unstable weather conditions over the next few days.
Assam and Meghalaya
Light to moderate rain may occur between May 12 and 14, accompanied by lightning and gusty winds of 30-50 km/h.Arunachal Pradesh
Rain and thunderstorm activity is expected on May 12 and 13.Nagaland, Manipur, Mizoram and Tripura
Lightning strikes and isolated thunderstorm activity may affect these states between May 11 and 14.Chhattisgarh Weather: Rain and Lightning Alert Issued
Chhattisgarh is expected to receive light to moderate rainfall at isolated places from May 11 to May 14. Thunderstorms, lightning strikes, and strong winds of up to 50 km/h may also occur in several districts.
Heavy Rainfall Alert for Kerala and Tamil Nadu
Southern states are likely to receive intense rainfall activity this week.
The IMD has issued a heavy rainfall alert for:
These regions may witness heavy showers at isolated locations between May 11 and May 16.
Lakshadweep and Karnataka May Receive Intense Rain
Lakshadweep is expected to witness heavy rainfall on May 11. Some parts of Karnataka may also experience rain and thunderstorm activity due to changing weather systems over the Arabian Sea region.
Weather Advisory for Residents
With thunderstorms, lightning, strong winds, and heavy rain predicted across several states, people are advised to:
- Take precautions while travelling in hilly and flood-prone regions
- Stay hydrated in heatwave-affected areas
The coming days are expected to bring highly dynamic weather conditions across India, making it important for residents to stay cautious and prepared.

articles.title: IMD warns heavy rain hailstorms and heatwave across several states - NewsBytes
articles.full_text:
IMD warns heavy rain hailstorms and heatwave across several states
The weather's about to get wild in many parts of the country.
The IMD says heavy rain is on the way for Tamil Nadu, Puducherry, South Interior Karnataka, Kerala, and Mahe between May 14-16 and May 15-16.
If you're up north in Jammu and Kashmir, Ladakh, Himachal Pradesh, or Uttarakhand, brace for possible hailstorms from May 11-12 in Jammu and Kashmir, Ladakh and Himachal Pradesh, and from May 12-13 in Uttarakhand.
Meanwhile, Rajasthan and Gujarat are set to sizzle with ongoing heatwave conditions.
Thunderstorms expected in Jharkhand and Odisha
Thunderstorms with lightning and gusty winds (up to 60km per hour) are expected this week in Jharkhand, Andaman and Nicobar Islands, Odisha, and Gangetic West Bengal.
Assam, Meghalaya, and Arunachal Pradesh could see heavy rainfall from May 13-17.
Rajasthan is looking at temperatures soaring up to 45 degrees Celsius to 47 degrees Celsius with dust storms likely soon.
Gujarat and western Madhya Pradesh will also stay hot for the next few days, so stay cool and keep an eye on local updates!
